title: ranunculus purshii var. gmelinii
intro: Ranunculus purshii var. gmelinii, also known as the western buttercup, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic due to the presence of compounds like ranunculin and protoanemonin. The health benefits of Ranunculus purshii var. gmelinii include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions like eczema and acne. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, which can be used externally to treat wounds and skin irritations.
